danger_signals.visual_form: First-person battle perspective shift; enemy sprites appear suddenly
danger_signals.meaning: Mansion spaces contain hostile supernatural threats; player safety is unstable

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite remains in location where death occurred
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent character death is possible; survival is fragile; team composition changes visually

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental decay visible in sprite work and tile layouts; structural damage
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate environmental danger; building will collapse; time pressure increases

danger_signals.visual_form: Spectral enemy design in first-person combat view; supernatural aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; emotional/supernatural danger distinct from physical threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Quick-time event prompts; visual hazard indicators in room layouts
danger_signals.meaning: Instantaneous death risk requiring reflexive response; architectural danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy sprite designs (zombies, ghosts, dolls) in combat encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Multiple threat types with different combat implications; unpredictability

safety_signals.visual_form: Item sprites scattered in rooms; collectible indicators
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety restoration; limited resource availability creates scarcity tension

safety_signals.visual_form: Five distinct item icons (camera, lighter, medical kit, lockpick, vacuum cleaner)
safety_signals.meaning: Team specialization creates safety through capability diversity; each role is essential

safety_signals.visual_form: Text overlays and cutscenes; document sprites in rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Environmental clues provide cognitive safety; puzzle solutions reduce uncertainty

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple characters visible together in exploration; visual party composition
safety_signals.meaning: Numbers provide safety; solo exploration is higher risk than group movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Metroidvania-style spatial layout; backtracking to known safe zones
safety_signals.meaning: Familiarity reduces danger; previously explored areas become relatively safe

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character sprites with distinct visual appearance (camera operator, nurse, etc.)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corpses mark team member locations after death; unique item placement indicates character progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Spectral ghost enemy design; distinct from physical zombies and dolls
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: First-person battle sprite; supernatural aesthetic differentiating from other enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Projection room reveals backstory; incinerator location marks tragic origin; presence throughout mansion

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied enemy types: zombies, ghosts, dolls each with distinct sprite design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy sprite variants appearing in different mansion locations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Random encounter system indicates pervasive threat; enemy presence normalized across all spaces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Closed doors; blocked passages; environmental barriers in tile layout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Team must split and explore to find escape route; forced exploration of all areas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Corpse sprite appears in location; character removed from active roster display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Permanent loss of character abilities; party composition changes; alternative items spawn near body

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character health meter depletes in battle; visual status change
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced scavenging for scattered tonics; vulnerability increases; death imminent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental decay intensifies; visual deterioration of room aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time pressure increases; escape imperative becomes urgent; final boss encounter triggered

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Final battle victory animation; ghost entity defeated in first-person view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescue sequence triggered; escape becomes possible; ending determined by survivor count

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Locked doors; blocked pathways; items cannot be used on environmental objects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Backtracking required; character switching for tool acquisition; spatial navigation puzzle

environmental_storytelling.example: Dead nurse Akiko leaves pill bottles nearby, allowing other characters to access healing without her presence; visual story of team adaptation

environmental_storytelling.technique: Scattered diary entries and secret messages
environmental_storytelling.example: Notes from past mansion visitors provide puzzle clues and narrative context; optional text reveals backstory of Mamiya family tragedy
Image of burning family displayed on projector communicates Lady Mamiya's tragic origin; visual evidence of motivating tragedy
Incinerator location marks the site of child death; spatial arrangement of rooms tells story of family life and suicide; interconnected layout mirrors exploration of psychological space
Camera, lighter, medical kit, lockpick, vacuum cleaner each represent filmmaker team specialization; visual identity tied to narrative role
Zombie, ghost, and doll enemies represent different supernatural threat categories; placement in mansion spaces suggests Mamiya's disturbed mental state (dolls as surrogate children)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can move independently or in parties of 2-3; can switch between characters; can pick up and drop items for other team members to retrieve
Permanently removed from roster; corpse remains visible in location; substitute items appear near body; team must adapt to lost capability
Menu-based combat initiated; first-person perspective battle; random encounter system means any space can trigger threat
Traps team in mansion; threatens all trespassers; initiates final confrontation in main chamber; defeat allows escape
Supports puzzle-solving; permits backtracking; allows character movement and item management
Structural danger increases; visual deterioration; time pressure escalates; forces final boss confrontation
Quick-time event triggered; instant death if response fails; forces reflexive player action

notes: Sweet Home (1989) is a foundational survival horror game that communicates world-state primarily through environmental decay, enemy encounter design, and irreversible character death. The game's visual language emphasizes vulnerability: health is scarce, death is permanent, and the mansion itself becomes increasingly hostile. Faction communication is minimal (protagonist filmmakers vs. supernatural threats) but the ghost antagonist Lady Mamiya is visually distinct from other enemies through spectral design. Environmental storytelling is sophisticated for the NES, using scattered documents, corpse placement, and spatial layout to reveal the tragic family history driving the haunting. The game's manifestation layer relies on contrast between safe exploration states and sudden danger triggers (random encounters, traps, structural collapse). Character death creates permanent visual changes—missing party members alter both combat capability and exploration options, making survival decisions have lasting aesthetic consequences. The five different endings based on survivor count create a meta-manifestation system where the visual roster becomes a narrative statement.
